name: Scottish Bannocks
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, oats, butter, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
2. Rub the mixture against the side of the bowl with a wooden spoon until the butter is completely blended in.
3. Slowly stir in enough milk to make stiff dough.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead just until it holds together.
5. Lightly flour the surface again.
6. Roll the dough into a sheet that is 1/3 inch thick.
7. Cut the dough into 2 1/2-inch rounds.
8. Gather any scraps together, reroll if necessary, and cut additional bannocks.
9. Arrange the bannocks on an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them about 1 inch apart.
10. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes, or until lightly browned.
11. Serve hot or cool on racks.
Makes 10 to 12 servings.
